answer:
**Allama Iqbal's poem "Falsafa-e-Gham" (The Philosophy of Sorrow)** appears as Chapter 7 in *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*. It is a complete verse exploration of sorrow's essential role in human life, love, spiritual awakening, and ultimate unity.[^1]

Here is the full English translation:

> [p-1ba46d] **The Philosophy of Sorrow**  
> 
> [p-141d8d] Though life's own wine is filled with joyful glee,  
> Its clouds hold tears for every soul to see.  
> Upon the waves of Sorrow, life's frail bubble rides,  
> A chapter of 'Pain' in life's great book abides.  
> A rose that has not lost a petal is no rose to see,  
> A nightingale that knows no autumn cannot truly be.  
> The heart's own story, with desire's blood, is stained,  
> The song of man is incomplete without a sorrowed strain.  
> For seeing eyes, a scar of Sorrow is the spirit's light,  
> A sigh becomes a mirror, making the soul bright.  
> Through trials of Sorrow, human nature finds its grace,  
> The dust of anguish is a polish for the heart's own face.  
> From pleasant dreams of youth, our Sorrow bids us rise,  
> The soul's own instrument awakes when this same plectrum plies.  
> For the heart's bird, Sorrow provides the wings for flight,  
> The heart's a secret; Sorrow brings that secret to the light.  
> Sorrow is not sorrow, but a silent song of soul,  
> Embracing all the music that makes existence whole.[^2]
> 
> [p-e436cc] The one whose dusk has never known a cry of 'Lord, I pray,'  
> Whose night is never lit by stars of tears that find their way;  
> Whose heart's own cup has never known the break that Sorrow brings,  
> Who stays forever drunk on wine that idle pleasure sings;  
> The hand that gathers flowers but has never felt a thorn,  
> Whose love knows not the agony of being left forlorn;  
> Though anguish may be absent from his nights and from his day,  
> The secret of all life is veiled and hidden far away.[^3]
> 
> [p-e693fc] O you who grasp the order that the universe displays,  
> Why should you not walk easily through Sorrow's trying maze?  
> Love is the preface to eternity's most ancient scroll,  
> While human Reason fades, Love is the ever-living soul.  
> Before the sun of Love, the dusk of death is put to shame,  
> Love is the fire of life itself, an everlasting flame.  
> If the beloved's parting meant Annihilation's art,  
> Then passion's fire would also flee the loving, grieving heart.  
> Love does not perish when the one beloved has passed away,  
> It lives within the soul as Sorrow, and is there to stay.  
> From Love's own Permanence, the loved one's being is secured,  
> Their life knows not oblivion, their presence is assured.[^4]
> 
> [p-811039] A river from the mountain's brow comes singing on its way,  
> And teaches birds of heaven songs to brighten up the day.  
> Its mirror shines as brightly as a heavenly maiden's face,  
> Then shatters on the valley rocks throughout that rugged space.  
> What was a stream becomes a thousand pearls of lovely sheen,  
> As from that fall, a million stars of water can be seen.  
> The flowing stream of mercury is scattered and undone,  
> A world of restless, anxious drops appears beneath the sun.  
> But Separation teaches every drop of Union's way,  
> Two steps ahead, the silver thread reforms without delay.  
> In truth, we are the flowing river of all life, you see,  
> Which, falling from a height, became the crowd of humanity.[^5]
> 
> [p-757295] We part within this lower world, to meet again one day,  
> And weep, believing that this parting's here to always stay.  
> The ones who die depart from sight, but are not swept away,  
> In truth, they never leave our side, not for a single day.[^6]
> 
> [p-523a9f] When Reason is besieged by all the troubles of the age,  
> Or lost within the darkened night upon youth's lonely stage;  
> When good and evil battle in the landscape of the heart,  
> And darkness on the path makes reaching one's true goal an art;  
> When Courage, our great guide, from all desire has turned aside,  
> When thought is rendered helpless, and the conscience has to hide;  
> When in life's valley not a single fellow-traveler's near,  
> No firefly's spark to light the path or make the way seem clear;  
> The brows of those who've passed are radiant in that deepest night,  
> The way the stars will always shine and fill the dark with light.[^7]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, [Falsafa-e-Gham](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/).

[^2]: Ibid., [p-1ba46d](#/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/#p-1ba46d) and [p-141d8d](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/#p-141d8d).

[^3]: Ibid., [p-e436cc](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/#p-e436cc).

[^4]: Ibid., [p-e693fc](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/#p-e693fc).

[^5]: Ibid., [p-811039](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/#p-811039).

[^6]: Ibid., [p-757295](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/#p-757295).

[^7]: Ibid., [p-523a9f](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/07-falsafa-e-gham/translation/#p-523a9f).
